definition: S&P 500 at ~21-22x forward P/E implicitly prices negative stock-bond correlation (lower required ERP, bonds still hedge), creating a cross-asset inconsistency with the potentially positive correlation regime. Under positive correlation pricing (higher required ERP of +50-100bp), fair value would be ~18-19x, implying 10-25% overvaluation — or the equity market correctly prices negative correlation reassertion.
evidence: S&P 500 at ~21-22x forward P/E more consistent with negative correlation pricing
Positive correlation requires higher ERP (+50-100bp), implying ~18-19x fair value
Debate pair_3: 'the single most valuable contrarian observation'
Cross-asset inconsistency of this magnitude should not persist — implies one market is wrong
open_questions: Is the equity market correctly pricing negative correlation reassertion, or is it genuinely overvalued?
How much of the valuation is explained by mega-cap tech concentration acting as a low-duration anchor?
What is the time horizon for resolution of this cross-asset inconsistency?
